title: Unable to communicate with own container port using host IP (CentOS 7, Podman 1.6.4)
body:
/kind bug


**Description**

A container with a port forward started on CentOS 7 with Podman 1.6.4 is unable to access it's own port using the host IP. Most likely related to an invalid Podman CNI bridge configuration (see also https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/6179).

Does it sound reasonable to graft the CNI configuration change also to older versions and make it available for CentOS7?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Start any container with a port forward, e.g.:
`podman run -it --rm -d -p 8080:80 --name web nginx`

2. Access the container port on the host node directly (response is successful), e.g.:
`curl <host_ip>:8080`


3. Access the container port from the container itself using the host IP (response unsuccessful, timeout occurs):
`podman exec -ti <container_id> curl <host_ip>:8080`


**Describe the results you received:**

The request from the container to itself using the host IP is unsuccessful (timeout).

**Describe the results you expected:**

The request from the container to itself using the host IP is successful.

**Additional information you deem important:**

Issue happens always on CentOS7 using Podman 1.6.4.  After investigating it seems related to Podman's CNI bridge configuration. The default configuration does not define `hairpinMode` (and hence, the default of `false`) applies.

It seems that the Podman CNI bridge plugin is properly configured since >v1.9 (relates to https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/6179). We have also applied to proper configuration on your CentOS 7 based systems and the issue has disappeared. 


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**

- Not tested the latest version of Podman, but it might work. However, latest version of Podman is not available for every distribution (e.g. CentOS 7).  
- Yes, checked the troubleshooting guide, but it did not contain any useful information for the reported issue.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

AWS EC2 running CentOS7

comments.body: We only support the latest version upstream. AFAIK RHEL 7 will only receive security updates for podman at this point so it is very unlikely that it will get fixed in RHEL/Centos 7.

Thanks @Luap99!

Does this ultimately mean Podman is not supported on RHEL/CentOS 7 (given that only official 1.6.4 Podman packages are available)?

It is up to the distros to decide what they support, we as upstream only support the latest versions.
https://www.redhat.com/en/blog/rhel-78-and-final-update-container-tools
